title: Tripleurospermum sannineum
taxonomy: Tripleurospermum sannineum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, Asteraceae. It is also known as the Syrian chamomile or Syrian mayweed. This plant is native to the Middle East and is often found in dry areas with poor soil. The exact classification of Tripleurospermum sannineum can be further broken down into its taxonomic hierarchy, which includes the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the order Asterales. Its classification is subject to ongoing research and possible revisions in the field of botany.

intro: Tripleurospermum sannineum, also known as Syrian chamomile or Syrian marigold, is a flowering plant species native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to its various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and improving sleep quality. The bioactive constituents of Tripleurospermum sannineum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to treat digestive issues, soothe skin irritations, and promote relaxation.
